title: Professional Networking Essentials
description: Build a genuine professional network that creates opportunities throughout your entire career.

modules.title: Networking Mindset
modules.description: Shift from transactional thinking to relationship building.

lessons.title: Give First: The Reciprocity Principle
lessons.content:
# Give First: The Reciprocity Principle

The most effective networkers are givers, not takers.

## What Giving Looks Like
- Sharing a relevant article with no expectation.
- Making an introduction between two people who would benefit.
- Offering your expertise or a quick answer to a question.
- Congratulating someone on a milestone.

## Why It Works
Reciprocity is one of the most powerful psychological principles. When you give first, people want to return the favour — and they remember you positively.

## The 5:1 Rule
For every ask you make, aim to give five times first. This builds relational credit before you need to draw on it.

## Practical Habit
Spend 10 minutes each morning doing one small act of professional generosity — a comment, a share, an introduction.

modules.title: Building Your Network
modules.description: Practical tactics for expanding your professional circle online and in person.

lessons.title: Crafting Connection Requests That Get Accepted
lessons.content:
# Crafting Connection Requests That Get Accepted

A blank connection request has a low acceptance rate. Personalise every one.

## The Three-Line Formula

1. **Context** — where you found them or what you have in common.
2. **Reason** — why you want to connect specifically.
3. **No ask** — do not ask for anything in the first message.

## Example

> Hi Sarah, I came across your article on supply-chain resilience and found your point about nearshoring really insightful. I am researching the same area and would love to connect and follow your future posts.

## Tips
- Reference something specific they posted or said.
- Keep it under 300 characters.
- Never paste the same message to everyone.

lessons.title: Making the Most of Networking Events

modules.title: Maintaining and Activating Your Network
modules.description: Keep relationships warm and know when to ask for support.

lessons.title: The Follow-Up System
lessons.content:
# The Follow-Up System

Most networking value is lost because people fail to follow up.

## After Meeting Someone
- Send a personalised message within 24 hours.
- Reference something specific from your conversation.
- Suggest a next step only if it is genuinely valuable for them.

## Staying in Touch
- Use a simple CRM (Notion, a spreadsheet, or HubSpot free tier).
- Tag contacts by industry, interest, and relationship strength.
- Set a reminder to reach out to key contacts every 90 days.

## Re-activating Dormant Contacts
A brief, genuine message works best:
> Hi James, I came across this podcast on sustainable logistics and immediately thought of our chat from last year. Hope you are well!

No agenda. Just a touch point.
